subagent_reports.topic: Design leverage points for adding /adv-optimizer read-only utility command

sources.label: adv-tron command (prompt-only utility, dedicated worker, read-only boundary)
sources.summary: Closest structural template: utility command, no CLI, spawns dedicated worker agent via Task tool with packet contract; read-only constraints + bounded findings + suggested next commands.

sources.label: adv-improve command (utility, prompt-only, evidence-backed, degraded execution)
sources.summary: Best fit for optimizer's evidence/proposal/degraded shape: Command Boundary, Exits (Report/Clarify/Partial), Target Resolution broad+scoped, evidence-required findings, Synthesis output, Context7/Exa fallback. Uses NO dedicated worker — orchestrator does scan inline.

sources.label: adv-slop-scan command (explore scanner fan-out, no-nested-delegation contract)
sources.summary: Canonical first-level-only delegation boundary text and explore-scanner fan-out pattern; scanners do work inline, no sub-agents, no /adv-* invocation. Directly reusable for SC5.

sources.label: manifest utility block
sources.summary: Utility command registration shape: phase:'utility', requiresChangeId:false, prerequisites:[], successors:['adv-proposal','adv-task']. adv-tron entry (405-413) is exact template for SC2.

sources.label: manifest.test.ts hard command-count assertion
sources.summary: Asserts exactly 26 commands AND an explicit expectedCommands list. Adding optimizer REQUIRES bumping 26->27 in two places (toHaveLength + count) and appending 'adv-optimizer' to the list, else test fails.

sources.label: manifest-doc-drift.test.ts (auto-covers new commands)
sources.summary: Generic loops: requires every manifest command to have a command doc + README + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS table row with EXACT description match, and frontmatter description 5-14 words. Auto-enforces SC1/SC3 once optimizer is registered — no new test code needed for drift.

sources.label: adv-improve-assets.test.ts (boundary/evidence/output test template)
sources.summary: Direct template for the optimizer-local asset test: Command Boundary section, Gate:None, no-ADV-state-mutation, forbids adv_change_create/adv_task_add/adv_gate_complete, Exits, evidence/output sections, degraded wording.

sources.label: adv-tron-assets.test.ts (minimal existence+fallback test)
sources.summary: Minimal asset-test shape if optimizer reuses an existing worker instead of new agent: existence + skill-fallback wording check.

sources.label: README + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS command tables
sources.summary: Two doc tables that drift test enforces; optimizer row must be appended with exact manifest description (SC3).

sources.label: agents directory (no local explore agent; adv-researcher IS local)
sources.summary: explore is a bundled/global OpenCode agent spawned by adv-review/adv-slop-scan without a repo-local file — so draft design's reuse of explore scanners is proven viable with zero new agent file. adv-researcher is repo-local and reusable for the best-practice/reference dimension.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Draft design direction (prompt-only command, reuse existing explore scanners + optional adv-researcher, optimizer-local asset test, update existing manifest/doc tests, no new spec) is strongly supported by existing patterns and satisfies C1/C2/C3. adv-improve is the closest sibling (utility + evidence + degraded + proposal output, no dedicated worker), and adv-slop-scan supplies the verbatim no-nested-delegation contract. The single highest-payoff non-obvious leverage point is that manifest-doc-drift.test.ts AUTO-enforces SC1/SC3 (existence + README/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS exact-description match) for free once optimizer is registered, while manifest.test.ts has a HARD-CODED count (26) + explicit list that WILL fail unless updated — this is the most likely missed-step. No new spec is needed: optimizer is command-only behavior, not capability law (matches adv-tron/adv-improve which have no spec).

validation.notes: All candidates stay within approved agreement (SC1-SC9, C1-C5, DONT1-DONT5). No candidate adds a new user-facing AC or alters the agreement. STOP_WHEN not triggered.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt prompt-only command modeled on adv-improve's boundary/exits/evidence/degraded shape + adv-slop-scan's explore fan-out and no-nested-delegation contract. Register in manifest utility block as exact clone of adv-tron entry (successors adv-proposal+adv-task). CRITICAL: update manifest.test.ts count 26->27 and append 'adv-optimizer' to expectedCommands or the suite fails. Write optimizer-local asset test cloned from adv-improve-assets.test.ts (boundary/no-mutation/no-create-tools/evidence/output/degraded). Rely on manifest-doc-drift.test.ts generic loops for SC1/SC3 (just add README + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS rows). No new spec; no new agent (reuse explore + optionally adv-researcher).
follow_ups: Design validator should confirm whether optimizer reuses explore scanners only or also adv-researcher for the best-practice/reference dimension (affects whether the asset test asserts a Task-tool spawn block like adv-tron vs inline-scan like adv-improve).
Confirm exact frontmatter description is 5-14 words (manifest-doc-drift caps it) and identical across manifest/frontmatter/README/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for /adv-optimizer read-only utility command

sources.label: manifest.test.ts hard command-count assertion
sources.summary: Asserts toHaveLength(26) AND an explicit expectedCommands list. Design Step 1 explicitly updates expected command list/count — correct; must bump 26->27 in both toHaveLength and the list, append 'adv-optimizer'.

sources.label: manifest.ts utility block (adv-tron template)
sources.summary: adv-tron entry is verbatim template for design D-registration: phase:'utility', requiresChangeId:false, prerequisites:[], successors:['adv-proposal','adv-task']. Design Step 3 matches exactly.

sources.label: adv-improve command (closest sibling: utility + evidence + degraded + proposal output, no dedicated worker)
sources.summary: Provides Command Boundary, Exits (Report/Clarify/Partial), evidence-required findings, synthesis output, Context7/Exa fallback, degraded annotation. Directly backs design SC4/SC6/SC7/SC8 sections and read-only boundary.

sources.label: adv-slop-scan no-nested-delegation contract + explore fan-out
sources.summary: Verbatim first-level-only delegation boundary: scanners inline, no sub-agents, no /adv-* invocation, packet contract with workdir/target, timeout=>partial, all-fail=>deterministic-only. Backs design D2/SC5/SC8/DONT2/DONT5.

sources.label: manifest-doc-drift.test.ts auto-enforcement
sources.summary: Generic loops auto-enforce SC1/SC3 (command doc + README + ADV_INSTRUCTIONS exact-description match, frontmatter 5-14 words) once optimizer is registered. Design DDC1 aligns; no new drift-test code needed.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is sound and well-grounded in existing patterns. Prompt-only command (D1), worker reuse via explore + optional adv-researcher (D2), proposal-shaped output not slop report (D3), and structural safety via optimizer asset test + existing manifest/doc drift tests (D4) all map directly to proven siblings: adv-improve for boundary/evidence/degraded/proposal shape, adv-slop-scan for explore fan-out + no-nested-delegation, adv-tron for manifest utility registration. No new spec required — optimizer is command-only behavior, not capability law, consistent with adv-tron/adv-improve which have no spec. Constraints C1 (no new agent), C2 (no CLI), C3 (not duplicating slop-scan: optimizer outputs simplification proposal, slop-scan outputs detector findings), C4 (structural tests), C5 (worktree) are all satisfiable within the design. One material logical gap: design Step 1/Step 3 do not commit to whether the asset test asserts a Task-tool spawn block (adv-tron style) versus inline-scan (adv-improve style); this maps to DDC3 (worker packet) which presumes a spawn block, while adv-improve performs scans inline. The design should make D2's fan-out concrete enough that the DDC3 packet-contract test has a real target. This is a clarity caution, not a blocker.

validation.notes: No spec-law conflict, no security/release blocker, no contract compromise. All design decisions stay within approved agreement (SC1-SC9, C1-C5, DONT1-DONT5). Verdict CAUTION due to one DDC3/D2 internal-consistency clarity gap, not a contract violation.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED with one CAUTION. The prompt-only design is the simplest viable approach and is strongly supported by adv-improve (boundary/evidence/degraded/proposal output), adv-slop-scan (explore fan-out + verbatim no-nested-delegation contract), and adv-tron (manifest utility registration). No materially simpler alternative exists short of dropping sub-agent fan-out entirely (which would weaken SC5/multi-perspective objective). No overlooked viable alternative — a CLI runner (C2) or dedicated agent (C1) would add scope the agreement explicitly defers. Resolve the single internal-consistency gap before planning: make D2 concrete about whether optimizer spawns a Task-tool worker packet (adv-tron style, satisfying DDC3's workdir/no-nested packet) or scans inline (adv-improve style); pick one so the DDC3 packet-contract asset test has a definite target. Also confirm the frontmatter description is exactly 5-14 words and byte-identical across manifest/frontmatter/README/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS (manifest-doc-drift enforces this).
follow_ups: Design should pin D2: explore Task-tool spawn (packet-contract per DDC3) vs inline scan (adv-improve style). DDC3 currently presumes a worker packet; reconcile.
Confirm exact 5-14 word frontmatter description identical across manifest/frontmatter/README/ADV_INSTRUCTIONS (DDC1; manifest-doc-drift.test.ts enforces).
Planning must bump manifest.test.ts toHaveLength(26)->(27) and append 'adv-optimizer' to expectedCommands or the suite fails.

items.text: Command existence: `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-optimizer` and a manifest comment declaring `requiresChangeId: false`.

Manifest registration: `plugin/src/manifest.ts` contains `adv-optimizer` with `phase: "utility"`, `requiresChangeId: false`, no gate, no prerequisites, and successors including `adv-proposal` and `adv-task`.
Documentation alignment: README and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` command tables include `/adv-optimizer` with exactly the manifest description.
Read-only boundary: command contract explicitly forbids code edits, ADV state mutation, agenda creation, task creation, and automatic deletion.
Delegation boundary: command contract explicitly allows only first-level worker agents and forbids scanner workers from spawning sub-agents or invoking `/adv-*` commands.
Evidence boundary: actionable findings require source evidence (`file:line`, symbol, metric, or cited source); evidence-free findings are omitted or marked non-actionable.

Proposal output: command contract requires an output section containing current state, ranked simplification opportunities, recommended long-term best-practice direction, risks, non-goals, and next ADV command.
Degraded execution: command contract defines partial-report behavior when some scanners fail/time out and deterministic-evidence-only behavior when all scanners fail.

Tests: targeted tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation for command presence, manifest count/alignment, frontmatter/manifest drift, doc-table drift, read-only boundary, no-nested-delegation boundary, and evidence/output requirements.
Do not create a new sub-agent unless design proves a dedicated worker is needed; use existing first-level agents where sufficient.
Do not add a CLI `bin/adv optimizer` runner in this change unless design proves deterministic runner scope is required for acceptance.
Do not duplicate `/adv-slop-scan`; optimizer must focus on simplification/refactor proposal synthesis, not slop-detector reporting.
Keep correctness structural: tests and manifest/doc drift checks must guard command registration and safety boundaries.
Implementation must happen in the ADV-managed `change/addOptimizerCommand` worktree.
No auto-deletion of code based on heuristic/sub-agent judgment.
No direct reads of ADV state files.
No broad unrelated refactor while adding the command.
No slash-command invocation from inside ADV orchestration.

documents.proposal:
# Add optimizer command

## Problem
ADV has reconnaissance (`/adv-tron`), improvement analysis (`/adv-improve`), and slop detection (`/adv-slop-scan`), but no focused optimizer command that asks sub-agents to identify bloated, overly verbose, overly complex, or under-optimized code and synthesize a complete simplification proposal.

## Scope
- Add `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md`.
- Register `adv-optimizer` in `plugin/src/manifest.ts` as a read-only utility command.
- Add/update command asset and manifest tests for command presence, metadata alignment, read-only boundary, first-level sub-agent limit, evidence requirements, and handoff toward tracked ADV work.
- Update `.adv/specs/*` only if discovery/design determines the command is capability law rather than command-only behavior.

## Out of Scope
- Direct code modification by `/adv-optimizer`.
- Automatic ADV change/task/agenda creation.
- Deletion of code based only on heuristic or sub-agent opinion.
- Replacing `/adv-slop-scan`, `/adv-improve`, or `/adv-tron`.

## Intended Command Behavior
`/adv-optimizer [target] [--json] [--depth light|standard|deep]` should:
1. Resolve target: repo, path, symbol, or concept.
2. Load project/spec context and relevant command boundaries.
3. Run deterministic local evidence gathering where practical: structure, complexity, duplication, churn/test adjacency when available.
4. Fan out to bounded first-level sub-agents for independent simplification scans.
5. Reject evidence-free findings and deduplicate overlap.
6. Emit a full simplification proposal: current-state evidence, ranked opportunities, recommended long-term best-practice direction, risks, explicit non-goals, and suggested next ADV command.

## Error Handling
- If target cannot be resolved: ask a focused clarification.
- If some scanners fail or time out: return a partial report with explicit coverage gaps.
- If all scanners fail: return deterministic evidence only, plus retry guidance.
- If a finding lacks source evidence: omit it from actionable recommendations.

## Acceptance Shape
- `/adv-optimizer` exists as a command and appears in manifest surfaces.
- Command contract requires source-backed findings and forbids mutation.
- Command contract enforces no nested sub-agent delegation.
- Tests prove command/manifest alignment and core safety boundaries.
documents.problemStatement: We need a dedicated ADV command that can proactively find unoptimized, bloated, overly verbose, or overly complex code and produce a full simplification proposal. Existing commands cover slop scanning, reconnaissance, and improvement analysis, but there is no focused optimizer workflow that coordinates sub-agents around simplification opportunities and turns findings into an evidence-backed, long-term best-practice proposal.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: Add optimizer command

## Objective
Add `/adv-optimizer` as a read-only ADV utility command that coordinates bounded first-level sub-agent analysis to identify simplification opportunities in source code and produce a complete, evidence-backed optimization/refactor proposal.

## Discovery Findings
- Existing adjacent commands cover partial needs:
  - `/adv-slop-scan` detects slop/deletion safety/detector coverage and already documents first-level-only scanner delegation.
  - `/adv-improve` produces evidence-backed improvement analysis but is broad and writes `docs/*-prep.md` research packs.
  - `/adv-tron` performs reconnaissance and suggestions but does not synthesize a simplification proposal.
- Command registration has enforced drift surfaces: `plugin/src/manifest.ts`, `.opencode/command/*.md`, README/ADV command tables, and manifest/doc tests.
- New command should be prompt-only initially; no CLI bridge unless a deterministic runner is added later.
- `adv_change_validate` passed; warnings `NO_TASKS` and `NO_DELTAS` are expected before planning.
- `adv_change_list` timed out twice despite healthy Temporal diagnostics, so related-work discovery used validation plus agenda sampling. No exact `/adv-optimizer` collision surfaced from gathered evidence.

## Success Criteria
- SC1: Command existence: `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-optimizer` and a manifest comment declaring `requiresChangeId: false`.
- SC2: Manifest registration: `plugin/src/manifest.ts` contains `adv-optimizer` with `phase: "utility"`, `requiresChangeId: false`, no gate, no prerequisites, and successors including `adv-proposal` and `adv-task`.
- SC3: Documentation alignment: README and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` command tables include `/adv-optimizer` with exactly the manifest description.
- SC4: Read-only boundary: command contract explicitly forbids code edits, ADV state mutation, agenda creation, task creation, and automatic deletion.
- SC5: Delegation boundary: command contract explicitly allows only first-level worker agents and forbids scanner workers from spawning sub-agents or invoking `/adv-*` commands.
- SC6: Evidence boundary: actionable findings require source evidence (`file:line`, symbol, metric, or cited source); evidence-free findings are omitted or marked non-actionable.
- SC7: Proposal output: command contract requires an output section containing current state, ranked simplification opportunities, recommended long-term best-practice direction, risks, non-goals, and next ADV command.
- SC8: Degraded execution: command contract defines partial-report behavior when some scanners fail/time out and deterministic-evidence-only behavior when all scanners fail.
- SC9: Tests: targeted tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation for command presence, manifest count/alignment, frontmatter/manifest drift, doc-table drift, read-only boundary, no-nested-delegation boundary, and evidence/output requirements.

## Constraints
- C1: Do not create a new sub-agent unless design proves a dedicated worker is needed; use existing first-level agents where sufficient.
- C2: Do not add a CLI `bin/adv optimizer` runner in this change unless design proves deterministic runner scope is required for acceptance.
- C3: Do not duplicate `/adv-slop-scan`; optimizer must focus on simplification/refactor proposal synthesis, not slop-detector reporting.
- C4: Keep correctness structural: tests and manifest/doc drift checks must guard command registration and safety boundaries.
- C5: Implementation must happen in the ADV-managed `change/addOptimizerCommand` worktree.

## Avoidances
- DONT1: No auto-deletion of code based on heuristic/sub-agent judgment.
- DONT2: No nested delegation.
- DONT3: No direct reads of ADV state files.
- DONT4: No broad unrelated refactor while adding the command.
- DONT5: No slash-command invocation from inside ADV orchestration.
documents.design:
# Design

## Architecture Overview
Add `/adv-optimizer` as a prompt-only ADV utility command, modeled after existing command contracts rather than a new runtime subsystem.

The command is a read-only orchestration contract that:
1. resolves a target repo/path/symbol/concept,
2. gathers local structural evidence,
3. fans out to bounded first-level `explore` scanner workers for independent simplification perspectives,
4. optionally uses `adv-researcher` inline/sub-agent research only for best-practice/reference evidence when needed,
5. rejects evidence-free recommendations,
6. synthesizes a full simplification proposal, and
7. hands off to `/adv-proposal` or `/adv-task` for tracked implementation.

No CLI runner, Temporal tool, new sub-agent, or spec delta is required for the first version.

## Key Decisions

### D1. Prompt-only command first
Use `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` plus manifest/docs/tests. Do not add `bin/adv optimizer`.

Rationale:
- Agreement C2 says no CLI runner unless required.
- Existing utility commands such as `/adv-improve` and `/adv-tron` prove prompt-only commands are supported.
- A deterministic optimizer runner would be a larger capability requiring separate detector schemas and likely spec law.

### D2. Use first-level `explore` scanner fan-out; no dedicated optimizer agent
The command owns orchestration and may spawn first-level `explore` scanners via the Task tool. Scanner prompts must include `WORKING DIRECTORY`, target/scope, expected output, and explicit bans on nested sub-agents and `/adv-*` slash commands. `adv-researcher` may be used only for a bounded best-practice/reference dimension when local findings need external validation.

Rationale:
- Agreement SC5 requires a delegation boundary; a concrete Task-tool scanner packet makes that testable.
- Agreement C1 forbids a new sub-agent unless proven necessary.
- `/adv-slop-scan` already establishes single-level scanner delegation language.
- `/adv-researcher` and `explore` cover best-practice and local-code scan needs without new agent contract overhead.

### D3. Optimizer synthesizes proposals, not findings-only slop reports
The command should distinguish itself from `/adv-slop-scan` by producing a proposal-shaped simplification plan: current state, ranked opportunities, target architecture, risks, non-goals, and next ADV command.

Rationale:
- Agreement C3 prohibits duplicating `/adv-slop-scan`.
- `/adv-slop-scan` owns detector coverage and smell taxonomy; optimizer owns maintainability/refactor proposal synthesis.

### D4. Structural safety via asset tests and existing drift tests
Use one optimizer-specific asset test for SC4-SC8 and update existing manifest/doc tests for SC1-SC3 and SC9.

Rationale:
- `manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` already enforces command doc, frontmatter, README, and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS drift.
- `manifest.test.ts` has a hard-coded command count/list and voice constraints.
- Optimizer-specific test can pin no-mutation, no-nested delegation, evidence, output, and degraded execution boundaries.

## ADR Drafts
None. Decisions are local command-contract additions, not hard-to-reverse architecture choices.

## Implementation Strategy

1. Add RED tests:
   - `plugin/src/adv-optimizer-assets.test.ts` asserting command exists and contains required safety/output sections.
   - Update `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts` expected command list/count from 26 to 27.
   - Existing `manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` should fail until command doc and doc tables align.
2. Add command contract:
   - `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` with frontmatter description exactly matching manifest.
   - Include target resolution, context loading, deterministic evidence gathering, first-level `explore` scanner fan-out, aggregation, report output, degraded execution, and constraints.
   - Include a scanner prompt packet with `WORKING DIRECTORY`, `TARGET`, `SCOPE`, `EXPECTED OUTPUT`, and no-nested/no-slash-command bans.
3. Register manifest entry:
   - `adv-optimizer` in `COMMAND_MANIFEST`, `phase: "utility"`, `requiresChangeId: false`, `prerequisites: []`, `successors: ["adv-proposal", "adv-task"]`.
   - Use description starting with an existing strong verb, e.g. `Analyze code simplification opportunities and propose optimizer changes`.
4. Update docs tables:
   - Add `/adv-optimizer [target]` row to `README.md` and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` with exact manifest description.
   - Update ADV_INSTRUCTIONS command-vs-skill table if needed to classify command as inline/prompt-only or command-specific embedded protocol.
5. Verify targeted suite through repo wrapper where appropriate.

## LBP Analysis
This is the least-broad long-term approach because it adds a command-level orchestration contract without prematurely creating a new runtime tool, CLI subcommand, sub-agent, or spec capability.

Industry-aligned maintainability principles applied:
- Keep behavior near owning command contract.
- Reuse existing scanners rather than multiplying agent types.
- Make safety boundaries test-backed.
- Separate discovery/proposal synthesis from code modification.
- Treat heuristics as advisory discovery signals, not correctness authority.

## Affected Components
- `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` — new command contract.
- `plugin/src/manifest.ts` — command registry.
- `plugin/src/manifest.test.ts` — expected command list/count.
- `plugin/src/adv-optimizer-assets.test.ts` — new asset test.
- `README.md` — command table row.
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` — command table row and optional command binding table row.
- `.opencode/token-budgets.json` — optional baseline if command token budget tracking is desired.

## Design-Derived Criteria
- DDC1: Command description must be 5-14 words, start with an allowed strong verb, and match exactly across manifest, command frontmatter, README, and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.
- DDC2: Optimizer command must explicitly state read-only behavior and forbid code edits, ADV mutations, agenda/task creation, and auto-deletion.
- DDC3: Worker packet section must include `WORKING DIRECTORY`, target/scope, expected output, and explicit no-nested/no-slash-command instructions for first-level `explore` scanners.
- DDC4: Report schema must separate actionable findings from low-confidence/user-review candidates.
- DDC5: Test coverage must be structural enough that deleting any required safety phrase or output section fails a targeted test.

## Risks / Mitigations
- Risk: Command becomes duplicate of `/adv-slop-scan`.
  - Mitigation: output is proposal-shaped and command text states detector reporting remains slop-scan-owned.
- Risk: Worker prompts create nested delegation.
  - Mitigation: copy single-level-only scanner contract pattern and test exact ban phrases.
- Risk: Manifest/doc drift.
  - Mitigation: rely on existing generic drift tests and update manifest hard-coded count/list.
- Risk: Overbuilding with CLI or new agent.
  - Mitigation: explicitly out of scope for this change; future deterministic runner can be a separate proposal if needed.

## Design Leverage Scout
Scout attempted with `adv-researcher`; result `VALIDATED` with five candidates.

Auto-adopted:
- Use `/adv-improve` as prompt-only utility template.
- Reuse `/adv-slop-scan` single-level delegation contract.
- Clone `/adv-tron` manifest utility shape.
- Update hard-coded `manifest.test.ts` command count/list.
- Let generic `manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` enforce README/ADV/frontmatter drift; add one optimizer-local asset test for safety boundaries.

Surfaced to user: none. No user-value tradeoff identified.

## Validator Result
Validator verdict: `CAUTION`.

Caution resolved in this design revision:
- Validator found D2/DDC3 did not pin whether optimizer used Task-tool scanner spawn or inline scan. Design now pins first-level `explore` scanner fan-out with a concrete worker packet, while keeping `adv-researcher` optional for best-practice/reference evidence.

Other validator findings:
- Prompt-only is the simplest viable path.
- No spec-law conflict found.
- CLI runner and dedicated agent were considered and correctly excluded by agreement constraints.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary: Add optimizer command

Added `/adv-optimizer` as a read-only ADV utility command for evidence-backed code simplification proposals.

## What Changed
- New command contract: `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md`.
- Manifest registration: `adv-optimizer` as a utility command with no gate and no change-id requirement.
- Documentation rows in README and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`.
- CLI surface matrix row marking the command as agent-workflow-only.
- New structural asset tests for optimizer command safety and output boundaries.
- Manifest/doc drift tests updated via command count/list alignment.

## Verified Outcomes
- Command exists and is registered.
- Command is read-only: no code edits, ADV mutations, agenda/task creation, automatic deletion, CLI runner, or dedicated optimizer agent.
- Command enforces first-level scanner delegation only.
- Command requires source-backed findings and separates actionable, user-review, and low-confidence recommendations.
- Command emits an `OPTIMIZER PROPOSAL` shape with current state, ranked opportunities, long-term direction, risks, non-goals, and next ADV command.

## Verification
- RED: optimizer asset test failed before command existed.
- GREEN/final: targeted Vitest suite passed: 66 tests across optimizer assets, manifest, manifest-doc drift, and CLI surface matrix.
- Static boundary check passed: no optimizer agent, no `bin/adv optimizer`, no ADV mutation tool names in optimizer command.
- Post-review cleanup passed: 24 targeted tests after removing duplicate state-mutation line.
- Independent reviewer final verdict: READY.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-26T18:16:23.750Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Command existence: `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` exists with frontmatter `name: adv-optimizer` and a manifest comment declaring `requiresChangeId: false`. | pass | `.opencode/command/adv-optimizer.md` exists with `name: adv-optimizer`; manifest-doc drift tests passed. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Manifest registration: `plugin/src/manifest.ts` contains `adv-optimizer` with `phase: "utility"`, `requiresChangeId: false`, no gate, no prerequisites, and successors including `adv-proposal` and `adv-task`. | pass | `plugin/src/manifest.ts` registers `adv-optimizer` as utility, requiresChangeId false, successors adv-proposal/adv-task; manifest tests passed. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Documentation alignment: README and `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` command tables include `/adv-optimizer` with exactly the manifest description. | pass | README and ADV_INSTRUCTIONS rows match manifest description; `manifest-doc-drift.test.ts` passed. |
| SC4 | success_criterion | Read-only boundary: command contract explicitly forbids code edits, ADV state mutation, agenda creation, task creation, and automatic deletion. | pass | Command boundary states `ADV State Mutation: none` and forbids code edits, ADV mutation, agenda/task creation, and automatic deletion; optimizer asset test passed. |
| SC5 | success_criterion | Delegation boundary: command contract explicitly allows only first-level worker agents and forbids scanner workers from spawning sub-agents or invoking `/adv-*` commands. | pass | Command includes `No Nested Scanner Delegation`, first-level scanner packet, and bans sub-agent spawning plus `/adv-*`; optimizer asset test passed. |
| SC6 | success_criterion | Evidence boundary: actionable findings require source evidence (`file:line`, symbol, metric, or cited source); evidence-free findings are omitted or marked non-actionable. | pass | Command includes `Source Evidence Requirement`, requires file/symbol/metric/citation evidence, and separates low-confidence/user-review/actionable groups; optimizer asset test passed. |
| SC7 | success_criterion | Proposal output: command contract requires an output section containing current state, ranked simplification opportunities, recommended long-term best-practice direction, risks, non-goals, and next ADV command. | pass | Command defines `OPTIMIZER PROPOSAL` with Current State, Ranked Simplification Opportunities, Recommended Long-Term Direction, Risks, Non-Goals, and Next ADV Command; optimizer asset test passed. |
| SC8 | success_criterion | Degraded execution: command contract defines partial-report behavior when some scanners fail/time out and deterministic-evidence-only behavior when all scanners fail. | pass | Command defines degraded execution for partial report, deterministic evidence only, and failed/timed-out scanners; optimizer asset test passed. |
| SC9 | success_criterion | Tests: targeted tests fail before implementation and pass after implementation for command presence, manifest count/alignment, frontmatter/manifest drift, doc-table drift, read-only boundary, no-nested-delegation boundary, and evidence/output requirements. | pass | RED failed on missing command; final targeted `pnpm exec vitest run src/adv-optimizer-assets.test.ts src/manifest.test.ts src/manifest-doc-drift.test.ts src/cli-surface-matrix.test.ts` passed 66 tests; cleanup rerun passed 24 tests. |
| C1 | constraint | Do not create a new sub-agent unless design proves a dedicated worker is needed; use existing first-level agents where sufficient. | respected | Static check confirms `.opencode/agents/adv-optimizer.md` does not exist; command uses existing `explore`/optional `adv-researcher`. |
| C2 | constraint | Do not add a CLI `bin/adv optimizer` runner in this change unless design proves deterministic runner scope is required for acceptance. | respected | Static check confirms `bin/adv` contains no optimizer surface. |
| C3 | constraint | Do not duplicate `/adv-slop-scan`; optimizer must focus on simplification/refactor proposal synthesis, not slop-detector reporting. | respected | Command states it does not replace `/adv-slop-scan`; output owns proposal synthesis, not detector reporting. |
| C4 | constraint | Keep correctness structural: tests and manifest/doc drift checks must guard command registration and safety boundaries. | respected | Added optimizer asset tests and updated generic manifest/doc/CLI matrix tests; targeted suites passed. |
| C5 | constraint | Implementation must happen in the ADV-managed `change/addOptimizerCommand` worktree. | respected | Implementation and checkpoints ran in ADV worktree `/home/jon/.local/share/opencode/worktree/.../change/addOptimizerCommand`. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | No auto-deletion of code based on heuristic/sub-agent judgment. | respected | Command explicitly forbids automatic deletion and says deletion candidates are recommendations for tracked follow-up review only. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | No nested delegation. | respected | Command includes `No Nested Scanner Delegation` and bans scanner workers from spawning additional sub-agents/delegates/workers. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | No direct reads of ADV state files. | respected | No ADV state files were read directly; ADV state accessed through ADV tools during workflow. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | No broad unrelated refactor while adding the command. | respected | Touched files limited to command/doc/test/manifest/CLI-matrix surfaces for optimizer; unrelated Markdown formatting churn was reverted. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | No slash-command invocation from inside ADV orchestration. | respected | No slash commands were invoked inside orchestration; command contract also bans scanner workers from invoking `/adv-*`. |
